name: Buddha's Delight
recipeIngredient: 2 Cakes pressed bean curd, or 6 fried gluten balls
recipeInstructions:
Preparation: Rinse, then soak dried ingredients in warm water: soak bean curd sticks overnight; soak the rest about 1 hour. Cut bean curd sticks and lily buds into 2-inch sections. Remove hard stems from wood ears, and slice thinly. Remove stems from black mushrooms (reserve for stock pot); halve the caps. Cut thin strands of bamboo shoots into 2-inch lengths. Cut soaked bean thread noodles into 3-inch pieces.
Wash and blanch bean sprouts, celery, and pepper. Slice celery, pepper, carrots, turnip, and cabbage into 2-inch long pencil-size pieces. Halve baby corn on the bias.
Slice pressed bean curd to match the vegetables' size. Deep-fry in shallow oil until slightly golden but still pliable. Drain.
Mash fermented bean curd, then blend with sugar, dark soy sauce, sherry, and water.
Stir-frying: Heat wok until medium-hot. Add 4 tablespoons of deep-frying oil. Add all dry ingredients except noodles; stir-fry for 1 minute. Turn wok to high heat. Add fresh and canned ingredients, and stir-fry for another 2 minutes, sprinkling in salt after the first minute. Add liquid mixture, mixing with juices in the pan. Add noodles. Reduce heat to medium, cover wok, and steam for 5 minutes, or until vegetables are cooked but still firm.
Uncover, sprinkle in MSG and sesame oil. Toss briefly. Serve in a warm bowl.
